title: Getting started with syncR
text: syncR integrator coordinator Circadia Lab R ecosystem. syncR::sync() — pulling everything alignment, just like SCN . package pulls three streams participant data single unified, participant-indexed database:

title: Getting started with syncR
text: result tidy, participant-indexed tibble one row per participant per night (applicable), columns supplied sources.
code: library(syncR)  db <- sync(   tallie = tallieR::export(),   slumb  = slumbR::export(),   zeit   = zeitR::export() )

title: Getting started with syncR
text: default sync() performs left join anchored first supplied source. Use join argument change :
code: # Only participants present in all three sources db_complete <- sync(   tallie = tallieR::export(),   slumb  = slumbR::export(),   zeit   = zeitR::export(),   join   = "inner" )  # All participants from any source, NAs where data is absent db_all <- sync(   tallie = tallieR::export(),   slumb  = slumbR::export(),   zeit   = zeitR::export(),   join   = "full" )

title: Getting started with syncR
text: data uses different participant identifier column name, pass via id_col:
code: db <- sync(   tallie = my_tallie_data,   zeit   = my_zeit_data,   id_col = "pid" )

title: syncR — Circadia Lab Data Integrator
text: syncR integrator coordinator Circadia Lab R ecosystem.

title: syncR — Circadia Lab Data Integrator
text: syncR provides single, unified entry point bringing together data across Circadia Lab package ecosystem. pulls sociodemographic questionnaire data tallieR, sleep diary data slumbR, actigraphy-derived circadian metrics zeitR one tidy, participant-indexed database. syncR::sync() — pulling everything alignment, just like SCN . Just suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) coordinates biological rhythms across body, syncR coordinates data streams across suite — making cross-domain analysis (actigraphy vs. subjective sleep quality, circadian phase vs. demographics) effortless.

title: syncR — Circadia Lab Data Integrator
text: 🔗 One function, three sources — sync() joins tallieR, slumbR, zeitR outputs single call 🧩 Flexible joining — left, inner, full join strategies suit study design 🪪 Participant-indexed — outputs keyed shared participant ID column 🧹 Tidy output — returns clean tibble ready modelling export 🔧 Source-agnostic — supply combination sources; omitted sources silently ignored

title: syncR — Circadia Lab Data Integrator
code: syncR/ ├── R/ │   ├── syncR-package.R         # Package-level documentation │   └── sync.R                  # Core sync() function ├── tests/ │   └── testthat/ │       └── test-sync.R         # Unit tests ├── vignettes/ │   └── syncR.Rmd               # Getting started vignette ├── .github/ │   └── workflows/ │       ├── R-CMD-check.yaml │       └── pkgdown.yaml ├── _pkgdown.yml └── DESCRIPTION

title: syncR — Circadia Lab Data Integrator
code: library(syncR)  db <- sync(   tallie = tallieR::export(),   # sociodemographics + questionnaires   slumb  = slumbR::export(),    # sleep diaries   zeit   = zeitR::export()      # actigraphy + circadian metrics )

title: syncR — Circadia Lab Data Integrator
code: # Inner join — only participants present in all sources db <- sync(tallie = ..., slumb = ..., zeit = ..., join = "inner")  # Full join — all participants, NAs where data is absent db <- sync(tallie = ..., slumb = ..., zeit = ..., join = "full")

title: syncR — Circadia Lab Data Integrator
text: 🌙 slumbR — sleep diary data collection processing ⏱️ zeitR — wrist actigraphy analysis circadian rhythm metrics 📋 tallieR — sociodemographic questionnaire data management 🔬 circadia-bio — Circadia Lab GitHub organisation

title: Synchronise data across the Circadia Lab package ecosystem — sync
text: sync() primary entry point syncR. pulls sociodemographic questionnaire data tallieR, sleep diary data slumbR, actigraphy-derived circadian metrics zeitR single unified, participant-indexed database. syncR::sync() — pulling everything alignment, just like SCN .

title: Synchronise data across the Circadia Lab package ecosystem — sync
code: sync(   tallie = NULL,   slumb = NULL,   zeit = NULL,   id_col = "participant_id",   join = c("left", "inner", "full") )

title: Synchronise data across the Circadia Lab package ecosystem — sync
text: tallie data frame sociodemographic / questionnaire data, typically output tallieR::export(). Pass NULL omit. slumb data frame sleep diary data, typically output slumbR::export(). Pass NULL omit. zeit data frame actigraphy / circadian metrics, typically output zeitR::export(). Pass NULL omit. id_col Character. Name participant ID column shared across input data frames. Defaults "participant_id". join Character. Type join perform combining sources. One "left" (default), "inner", "full".

title: Synchronise data across the Circadia Lab package ecosystem — sync
text: tibble one row per participant per night (applicable), containing columns supplied sources, indexed id_col.
